fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 34-year-old African American female with schizoaffective disorder presenting in crisis.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Williams brought to emergency evaluation by crisis team after neighbors called 911 hearing her screaming and breaking objects. Mother provides collateral reporting patient completely stopped olanzapine 2 months ago stating medications were 'government mind control.' Since then, catastrophic deterioration. Reports constant auditory and visual hallucinations - hearing voices commanding her to harm herself, seeing demons in apartment. Endorses elaborate delusions about CIA tracking her through implanted chips. Hasn't slept more than 2 hours nightly in weeks per mother. Refusing most food believing it's poisoned, weight down 32 lbs to 108 lbs, severely malnourished appearance. Lost job 6 weeks ago after not showing up. Eviction notice received yesterday. Apartment in deplorable condition - no utilities, bizarre writings on walls, trash everywhere. No self-care for weeks. Mood 'they're coming for me' rated 0/10. Mother cannot manage at home. Safety: active SI with plan to jump from balcony to 'escape demons,' command hallucinations to self-harm, denies HI. (Evaluated by Dr. Angela Thompson, ED Physician)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Severely malnourished, malodorous, disheveled, appears acutely psychotic and terrified
Mental_Status_Examination.Speech: Disorganized, loud at times, responding to internal stimuli
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Severe paranoid delusions, command auditory hallucinations to self-harm, active SI with plan, visual hallucinations

Behavioral_Health_Checkup.C_SSRS: Active suicidal ideation with plan and intent; command hallucinations

fields.Current_Medications: Olanzapine 15 mg PO qHS (stopped 2 months ago by patient)

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Severe malnutrition markers; albumin 2.4 g/dL; electrolyte abnormalities; glucose 78 mg/dL.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective disorder with severe psychotic and suicidal crisis secondary to complete medication discontinuation; grave disability; danger to self.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Recently evicted; recently unemployed; no utilities; severe self-neglect; complete functional collapse.
Assessment_and_Plan: Life-threatening crisis after stopping olanzapine 2 months ago.
Active SI with plan, command hallucinations, severe malnutrition.
Meets criteria for involuntary hospitalization - danger to self and grave disability.
Requires immediate inpatient admission to medical psychiatry unit.
Will restart olanzapine with close monitoring.
Social work for housing, disability benefits post-discharge.
May require board and care placement after stabilization.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Angela Thompson, ED Physician — County Emergency Department, 1000 Emergency Plaza, Los Angeles, CA 90015, Phone: (555) 992-4487, Fax: (555) 992-4488
